definition: (of body tissue) used for the storage of fat.
Adipose tissue stores energy in the body.
helping or assisting, especially in a medical context
Adjuvant therapy is often used after surgery to prevent recurrence.
(of the mouth) wide open, especially with surprise or wonder
His mouth was agape in astonishment at the magic trick.
Relating to or adapted for walking; able to walk.
The patient was ambulatory after the surgery.
relating to the amnion or amniotic fluid
The baby was surrounded by amniotic fluid.
relating to or promoting anabolism (the synthesis of complex molecules in living organisms).
Anabolic steroids are used to build muscle mass.
originating in human activity
Climate change is largely driven by anthropogenic factors.
having human characteristics
Many ancient gods were depicted as anthropomorphic figures.
concerned with or dealing with applications for decisions to be reversed
The case went to the appellate court.
relating to astrophysics
They conducted an astrophysical study of distant galaxies.
relating to or approaching an asymptote (a line that a curve approaches as it heads towards infinity)
The graph shows an asymptotic curve approaching the x-axis.
relating to an atrium, especially of the heart
The patient was diagnosed with atrial fibrillation.
relating to a chromosome that is not a sex chromosome
Autosomal dominant inheritance means only one copy of an altered gene is needed to cause the disorder.
relating to a carboxylic acid
Acetic acid is a simple carboxylic acid.
having a non-superimposable mirror image
Many biological molecules are chiral, existing as left- or right-handed forms.
relating to the cochlea of the inner ear
A cochlear implant can help people with severe hearing loss.
naturally accompanying or associated
Loss of memory is a concomitant symptom of the disease.
relating to or involving convection
Convective currents in the atmosphere can lead to thunderstorms.
relating to the Copts, a Christian group in Egypt
The museum displayed ancient Coptic manuscripts.
relating to or involving the production of very low temperatures
The samples were stored in a cryogenic freezer.
relating to or affecting the skin
The rash caused a severe cutaneous reaction.
relating to the cytoplasm of a cell
The cytoplasmic membrane plays a crucial role in cell function.
relating to or resembling a dendrite or tree-like structure
The neuron has a complex dendritic structure.
relating to the philosophical concept of dialectic; concerned with or acting through opposing forces
Her argument was based on a complex dialectical process.
having the property of being an electrical insulator
Rubber is a good dielectric material.
relating to the church
He studied ecclesiastical history.
outstandingly bad; shocking
The egregious error in the report led to serious consequences.
relating to electrochemistry
The battery relies on an electrochemical reaction to produce power.
having an internal cause or origin
The plant produces endogenous hormones.
relating to the endothelium
Endothelial cells line the inside of blood vessels.
relating to or arising from nongenetic influences on gene expression
Epigenetic changes can affect how genes are expressed without altering the DNA sequence.
intended for or likely to be understood by only a small number of people with a specialized knowledge or interest
The book was full of esoteric references that only a few scholars could understand.
relating to the scientific description of peoples and cultures
The anthropologist conducted extensive ethnographic research.
originating from outside the organism
The plant's growth was influenced by exogenous factors like sunlight.
(of an action or procedure) occurring or executed without legal authority
The report detailed several cases of extrajudicial killings.
involving trust, especially with regard to the relationship between a trustee and a beneficiary
A lawyer has a fiduciary duty to their clients.
relating to genocide
The regime was accused of genocidal acts.
relating to geodesy, the science of measuring the Earth's shape
Geodetic surveys are crucial for accurate mapping.
relating to geology and engineering
The company specializes in geotechnical engineering for large construction projects.
relating to knowledge, especially mystical or spiritual knowledge
Ancient Gnostic texts offer a different perspective on early Christianity.
ruling or dominant in a political or social context
The country sought to establish its hegemonic influence in the region.
relating to or causing hemorrhage
The patient was diagnosed with hemorrhagic fever.
relating to the liver
The doctor ordered tests to check his hepatic function.
enabling a person to discover or learn something for themselves
The teacher used a heuristic approach to problem-solving.
relating to the study of the microscopic structure of tissues
The biopsy required a detailed histological examination.
relating to or denoting any disease or condition which arises spontaneously or for which the cause is unknown
The doctor diagnosed her with idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, meaning the cause was unknown.
relating to inertia
The spacecraft used an inertial guidance system to navigate.
relating to or situated in the interstices (small spaces)
Interstitial fluid fills the spaces between cells in the body.
relating to or denoting ischemia (an inadequate blood supply to an organ or part of the body)
The patient suffered an ischemic stroke due to a blocked artery.
relating to isotopes
Scientists use isotopic analysis to determine the age of ancient artifacts.
harmful or evil in intent or effect
The sorceress cast a maleficent spell on the kingdom.
relating to mitochondria
Mitochondrial DNA is inherited from the mother.
relating to the mitral valve of the heart
The patient was diagnosed with mitral valve prolapse.
Derived from a single cell clone
Scientists are developing new monoclonal antibodies for cancer treatment.
relating to the form or structure of things
Linguists study the morphological changes in languages over time.
involving or depending on multiple variables
The study used multivariate analysis to examine the complex data.
relating to or denoting the muscles and skeleton
The doctor specialized in musculoskeletal disorders.
relating to or resembling bone marrow
Acute myeloid leukemia is a type of cancer of the blood and bone marrow.
relating to the nature of being
The philosopher discussed the ontological implications of existence.
relating to or involving oxidation
Oxidative stress can damage cells.
of, relating to, or forming the wall of a body part or cavity.
The parietal lobe is involved in processing sensory information.
relating to the breast or chest
He worked on his pectoral muscles at the gym.
relating to teaching
The new curriculum has a strong pedagogical foundation.
relating to pharmacology or drugs
The study investigated the pharmacological effects of the new compound.
relating to the evolutionary development and diversification of a species or group of organisms
Scientists use phylogenetic analysis to understand the relationships between different species.
relating to the generation of electricity by pressure
Piezoelectric materials are used in many sensors and transducers.
relating to a plane; flat
The design involved complex planar surfaces.
relating to the sole of the foot
He suffered from plantar fasciitis, an inflammation of the foot.
operated by air or gas under pressure
The factory uses pneumatic tools for assembly.
Relating to or involving probability.
The weather forecast uses a probabilistic model to predict rain.
intended to prevent disease
The doctor prescribed a prophylactic antibiotic to prevent infection.
seemingly; apparently but not really
The organization operates as a quasi-governmental body.
rare, exotic, or obscure
She had a penchant for recherche wines.
relating to or denoting an organism, cell, or genetic material that has been recombined
Scientists are working with recombinant DNA technology.
relating to rheumatism or arthritis
She suffers from rheumatoid arthritis.
relating to or situated on the banks of a river
The conservation efforts focused on protecting the riparian habitats.
inciting or causing people to rebel against authority
The government arrested him for making seditious remarks.
Strongly influencing later developments; highly original and influential.
His seminal work on quantum physics changed the field forever.
Covered with or consisting of scales.
The doctor identified squamous cell carcinoma.
randomly determined; involving a random variable
The model uses stochastic processes to predict market fluctuations.
relating to stratigraphy (the study of rock layers)
Geologists use stratigraphic analysis to understand Earth's history.
relating to taxonomy (the classification of organisms)
The taxonomic classification of this species is still debated.
relating to or using nuclear reactions that occur at extremely high temperatures.
Scientists are researching controlled thermonuclear fusion as a future energy source.
relating to topology, the branch of mathematics concerned with the properties of geometric objects that are preserved under continuous deformations
A donut and a coffee cup are topologically equivalent.
Beyond normal or physical human experience.
He sought a transcendental experience through meditation.
relating to transcription
The scientists studied the transcriptional regulation of the gene.
containing genes from another species
Researchers are developing transgenic crops to resist pests.
a light, porous rock formed by the consolidation of volcanic ash
The ancient city was carved directly into the volcanic tuff.
